(born October 28, 1986) is an American [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer"). He challenged for the [WBO](/wiki/WBO "WBO") [super featherweight](/wiki/Super_featherweight "Super featherweight") title in 2013 and the WBO [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") title in 2015. At regional level, he held the [WBC](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council")-[NABF](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Federation "North American Boxing Federation") super featherweight title from 2011 to 2012.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He's also the brother of [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [Jesús Magdaleno](/wiki/Jes%C3%BAs_Magdaleno "Jesús Magdaleno").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He has been ranked in the top ten of [*The Ring* magazine](/wiki/The_Ring_(magazine) "The Ring (magazine)")'s super featherweight ratings.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

first_paragraph: **Diego Armando Magdaleno** (born October 28, 1986) is an American [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer"). He challenged for the [WBO](/wiki/WBO "WBO") [super featherweight](/wiki/Super_featherweight "Super featherweight") title in 2013 and the WBO [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") title in 2015. At regional level, he held the [WBC](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council")-[NABF](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Federation "North American Boxing Federation") super featherweight title from 2011 to 2012.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He's also the brother of [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [Jesús Magdaleno](/wiki/Jes%C3%BAs_Magdaleno "Jesús Magdaleno").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He has been ranked in the top ten of [*The Ring* magazine](/wiki/The_Ring_(magazine) "The Ring (magazine)")'s super featherweight ratings.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**Diego Armando Magdaleno** (born October 28, 1986) is an American [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer"). He challenged for the [WBO](/wiki/WBO "WBO") [super featherweight](/wiki/Super_featherweight "Super featherweight") title in 2013 and the WBO [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") title in 2015. At regional level, he held the [WBC](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council")-[NABF](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Federation "North American Boxing Federation") super featherweight title from 2011 to 2012.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He's also the brother of [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [Jesús Magdaleno](/wiki/Jes%C3%BAs_Magdaleno "Jesús Magdaleno").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He has been ranked in the top ten of [*The Ring* magazine](/wiki/The_Ring_(magazine) "The Ring (magazine)")'s super featherweight ratings.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diego_Magdaleno&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

He was named after [Diego Maradona](/wiki/Diego_Maradona "Diego Maradona") and is the brother of [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [Jesús Magdaleno](/wiki/Jes%C3%BAs_Magdaleno "Jesús Magdaleno").

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diego_Magdaleno&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional career")]

He is signed to [Bob Arum](/wiki/Bob_Arum "Bob Arum")'s [Top Rank](/wiki/Top_Rank "Top Rank").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
In April 2010, Diego won a ten-round decision over [Manuel Perez](/wiki/Manuel_Perez_(boxer) "Manuel Perez (boxer)")[[6]](#cite_note-6)

After receiving a top ten [Super Featherweight](/wiki/Super_Featherweight "Super Featherweight") ranking by the [Ring Magazine](/wiki/Ring_Magazine "Ring Magazine"), Magdaleno beat contender [Alejandro Pérez](/wiki/Alejandro_P%C3%A9rez_(boxer) "Alejandro Pérez (boxer)").[[7]](#cite_note-7)
